title: Terms and Conditions
intro: These terms govern your access to flyair.lk (the "Site") and to the booking and support services we provide. By using the Site you accept these terms in full. Please read them carefully — if anything is unclear, contact us before you book.

sections.p:
FlyAir is the flights-focused brand of Travellers Marketplace (Pvt) Ltd — a Sri Lankan company incorporated in 2019 under company registration number PV 00214561, with a team that has been working in the travel industry since 2000. The parent company is licensed by the Sri Lanka Civil Aviation Authority (CAA licence A-1710) and is an IATA accredited travel agent.

flyair.lk sits in a family of Travellers Marketplace brands alongside travellers.lk (full-service travel) and Travellers Holidays (tour packages and curated trips). The head office is in Colombo, with branches in Kandy, Nuwara Eliya and Kurunegala.

Throughout this document "FlyAir", "we", "us" and "our" refer to the FlyAir brand and to its operator, Travellers Marketplace (Pvt) Ltd. "You" and "your" refer to the person using the Site or making a booking.

You must be at least 18 years old and legally able to enter binding contracts to use FlyAir. By placing a booking you confirm that any information you provide is accurate and that you have authority to act for anyone you are booking for.

Where you choose to create an account, you are responsible for keeping your login details confidential and for any activity that takes place under your account. Please tell us straight away if you believe your account has been used without your permission.

FlyAir is not an airline and does not itself operate flights. We display fares and availability supplied to us by carriers and global distribution systems and pass your booking request through to them.

The contract for your flight is between you and the operating airline. The airline's own conditions of carriage govern that contract and may include limits on liability, change rules and other terms you should review.

Fares are shown in the currency you select and include the base fare, applicable taxes and fees as displayed at the time of booking. Airline-collected fees (for example checked baggage, seat selection or meals) may apply additionally and are paid to the airline.

Fares are not held until ticketed. If a fare becomes unavailable or is found to be incorrectly displayed before we issue your ticket, we will contact you to offer the corrected fare or a full refund of any amount taken.

We accept the payment methods shown at checkout. Your payment is processed by a regulated payment provider; FlyAir does not store full card numbers on its own systems.

By submitting payment you authorise us to charge the total displayed. Some banks may apply currency conversion or international transaction fees that are outside our control.

A booking is confirmed only when you receive a confirmation email containing an airline reservation reference and, where applicable, a ticket number. The on-screen receipt that follows checkout is acknowledgement of payment, not of ticketing.

If we are unable to issue the ticket — for example because the fare or seat is no longer available — we will let you know and refund you in full. We are not liable for any consequential loss in that situation.

Whether you can change or cancel a booking, and what it will cost, depends on the fare rules of the ticket you bought. Most discounted economy fares are non-refundable and carry change fees; premium fares are usually more flexible.

Where a change is permitted, the airline's change fee plus any fare difference applies, and FlyAir may charge a small service fee for handling the change. Cancellations after a flight has departed are generally not refundable.

sections.h: Changes and cancellations by the airline
sections.p:
Airlines occasionally change schedules, retime flights or cancel services. When this happens we will contact you using the details provided on your booking and help you with rebooking or refund options offered by the airline.

FlyAir's role is to facilitate the airline's resolution; the entitlement (rebooking, refund, compensation) is set by the airline and applicable passenger-rights regulations, not by FlyAir.

Refunds are processed back to the original payment method. The timeline depends on the airline's processing and your bank or card issuer — typically between 7 and 28 working days from the date the airline releases the refund to us.

Where a service fee was charged by FlyAir for handling a change or cancellation, that fee is generally non-refundable.

It is your responsibility to ensure you hold a valid passport, any required visas, transit visas and other travel documents for your full itinerary, and that they meet the validity rules of every country you enter or transit through.

Airlines may deny boarding if your documents are not in order. FlyAir is not liable for refused boarding, denied entry or expenses incurred as a result of inadequate travel documents. We can assist with visa guidance and document checklists through our visa services team.

Health entry requirements, vaccination certificates and government travel advisories can change at short notice. We recommend checking the official advice for your origin, destination and any transit country before you book and again before you travel.

We strongly recommend taking out comprehensive travel insurance that covers cancellation, medical care, baggage and disruption — insurance options can be added to most bookings.

Cabin and checked baggage allowances, including weight and dimension limits, are set by the operating airline. The allowance shown in your booking is provided by the airline at the time of issue and may be subject to additional fees if exceeded at the airport.

If your itinerary involves multiple airlines, the most restrictive allowance generally applies. Please check each carrier's published policy before travel.

You should arrive at the airport in good time — generally at least 3 hours before departure for international flights and 2 hours for domestic flights, unless the airline advises otherwise.

Online check-in availability and timings vary by airline. Some carriers charge a fee for airport check-in or boarding passes printed at the airport.

Neither FlyAir nor any airline is liable for delay or failure to perform that is caused by events beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, war, civil unrest, government action, strikes, public health emergencies, air traffic restrictions and severe weather.

In such situations we will assist you in good faith to obtain the remedies made available by the airline and applicable law.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, FlyAir's total liability to you arising from your use of the Site or from any booking is limited to the service fees we received from you for the relevant booking.

We are not liable for indirect or consequential losses, including loss of profit, loss of enjoyment, or missed connections caused by events outside our reasonable control. Nothing in these terms excludes liability for fraud or for death or personal injury caused by negligence.

The FlyAir name, the FlyAir logo, the site design, the underlying code and all editorial content on flyair.lk are owned by Travellers Marketplace (Pvt) Ltd or its licensors and are protected by copyright and trademark laws.

You may view and print pages for your personal, non-commercial use related to a booking you have made or are planning. Any other reproduction, scraping or redistribution requires our prior written permission.

If you submit reviews, ratings or other content to the Site, you grant FlyAir a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide licence to display, store and republish that content in connection with the Site and our marketing channels.

You are responsible for what you submit. Content that is unlawful, defamatory, hateful, infringing or that contains personal data of other people without their consent will be removed.

You agree not to use the Site to make speculative, false or fraudulent bookings; to interfere with the operation of the Site; to scrape fares or content; to bypass security; or in any way that breaches applicable law.

We reserve the right to refuse service, cancel bookings or close accounts where we reasonably believe these terms have been breached.
How we collect, use and protect personal information is described in our Privacy Policy, which forms part of these terms.

By giving us your contact details you agree that we may contact you with operational messages about your booking by email, SMS, WhatsApp or phone, and that we may share these details with the operating airline where this is necessary to deliver your travel.

Marketing messages are optional. You can opt in or out at any time from your account or by using the unsubscribe link in the relevant message.

These terms are governed by the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka. Any dispute that cannot be resolved by good-faith discussion will be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Sri Lanka.

If any part of these terms is held to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect.

We may update these terms from time to time to reflect changes in our service or in applicable law. The current version is always available on this page, with the date of the last update shown above.

Continuing to use the Site after an update means you accept the revised terms; changes do not apply retroactively to bookings already confirmed.

If you have any question about these terms or about a booking, please reach our team at info@flyair.lk or on 0788 788 788.

Travellers Marketplace (Pvt) Ltd · Head office: Colombo, Sri Lanka · Branches: Kandy, Nuwara Eliya, Kurunegala · CAA Licence A-1710 · BR No PV 00214561.
